reasoning: The informal statement says that if f : ℕ → ℕ is injective, then the limit of the sequence (p_n) is unaffected by the rearrangement q_k = p_{f(k)}. The Lean statement captures exactly this: given an injective f : ℕ → ℕ and a sequence p : ℕ → α with Tendsto p atTop (𝓝 a), it concludes Tendsto (λ n => p (f n)) atTop (𝓝 a), which says the rearranged sequence converges to the same limit. The hypothesis "Injective f" matches "f is an injection", the rearrangement p ∘ f matches q_k = p_{f(k)}, and preserving the limit matches "unaffected by rearrangement". The informal statement does not specify the codomain of the sequence, so formalizing over an arbitrary topological space α is the natural general setting where "limit of a sequence" is defined (via Tendsto ... (𝓝 a)). The theorem is indeed true in this generality (since `Function.Injective.nat_tendsto_atTop` ensures f tends to infinity at infinity), so this is a faithful encoding of the mathematical content.
